steps: Let the width be \(w\) cm. The problem states the length is 41 cm greater than the width, so length \(= w + 41\).
explanation:
• Choice A — '\(A=(w)\left(\frac{w}{41}\right)\)': This makes length \(= w/41\), a fraction of the width — the opposite relationship from what is stated. ✗
  • Choice B — '\(A=(w)(41w)\)': This makes length \(= 41w\), meaning 41 times the width, not 41 more than the width. ✗
  • Choice C — '\(A=(w)(w+41)\)': Length \(= w + 41\) (41 greater than the width); area \(= w(w+41)\). This matches exactly. ✓
  • Choice D — '\(A=w^2\)': This requires length \(= w\), i.e., a square. But the paving stone is not a square — the length exceeds the width by 41 cm. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
